337: #ifdef FEAT_EVAL
338: static char_u	*do_one_cmd(char_u **, int, cstack_T *, char_u *(*fgetline)(int, void *, int, getline_opt_T), void *cookie);
339: #else
340: static char_u	*do_one_cmd(char_u **, int, char_u *(*fgetline)(int, void *, int, getline_opt_T), void *cookie);
341: static int	if_level = 0;		// depth in :if
342: #endif
343: static void	append_command(char_u *cmd);
344: 
345:     static void
346: append_command(char_u *cmd)
347: {
348:     char_u *s = cmd;
349:     char_u *d;
350: 
351:     STRCAT(IObuff, ": ");
352:     d = IObuff + STRLEN(IObuff);
353:     while (*s != NUL && d - IObuff + 5 < IOSIZE)
354:     {
355: 	if (enc_utf8 ? (s[0] == 0xc2 && s[1] == 0xa0) : *s == 0xa0)
356: 	{
357: 	    s += enc_utf8 ? 2 : 1;
358: 	    STRCPY(d, "<a0>");
359: 	    d += 4;
360: 	}
361: 	else if (d - IObuff + (*mb_ptr2len)(s) + 1 >= IOSIZE)
362: 	    break;
363: 	else
364: 	    MB_COPY_CHAR(s, d);
365:     }
366:     *d = NUL;
367: }
368: 
369:     static char_u *
370: do_one_cmd(
371:     char_u	**cmdlinep,
372:     int		flags,
373: #ifdef FEAT_EVAL
374:     cstack_T	*cstack,
375: #endif
376:     char_u	*(*fgetline)(int, void *, int, getline_opt_T),
377:     void	*cookie)		// argument for fgetline()
378: {
379:     char_u	*p;
380:     linenr_T	lnum;
381:     long	n;
382:     char	*errormsg = NULL;	// error message
383:     char_u	*after_modifier = NULL;
384:     exarg_T	ea;			// Ex command arguments
385:     cmdmod_T	save_cmdmod;
386:     int		save_reg_executing = reg_executing;
387:     int		save_pending_end_reg_executing = pending_end_reg_executing;
388:     int		ni;			// set when Not Implemented
389:     char_u	*cmd;
390:     int		starts_with_colon = FALSE;
391: #ifdef FEAT_EVAL
392:     int		may_have_range;
393:     int		vim9script;
394:     int		did_set_expr_line = FALSE;
395: #endif
396:     int		sourcing = flags & DOCMD_VERBOSE;
397: 
398:     CLEAR_FIELD(ea);
399:     ea.line1 = 1;
400:     ea.line2 = 1;
401: #ifdef FEAT_EVAL
402:     ++ex_nesting_level;
403: #endif
404: 
405:     // When the last file has not been edited :q has to be typed twice.
406:     if (quitmore
407: #ifdef FEAT_EVAL
408: 	    // avoid that a function call in 'statusline' does this
409: 	    && !getline_equal(fgetline, cookie, get_func_line)
410: #endif
411: 	    // avoid that an autocommand, e.g. QuitPre, does this
412: 	    && !getline_equal(fgetline, cookie, getnextac))
413: 	--quitmore;
414: 
415:     /*
416:      * Reset browse, confirm, etc..  They are restored when returning, for
417:      * recursive calls.
418:      */
419:     save_cmdmod = cmdmod;
420: 
421:     // "#!anything" is handled like a comment.
422:     if ((*cmdlinep)[0] == '#' && (*cmdlinep)[1] == '!')
423: 	goto doend;
424: 
425: /*
426:  * 1. Skip comment lines and leading white space and colons.
427:  * 2. Handle command modifiers.
428:  */
429:     // The "ea" structure holds the arguments that can be used.
430:     ea.cmd = *cmdlinep;
431:     ea.cmdlinep = cmdlinep;
432:     ea.getline = fgetline;
433:     ea.cookie = cookie;
434: #ifdef FEAT_EVAL
435:     ea.cstack = cstack;
436:     starts_with_colon = *skipwhite(ea.cmd) == ':';
437: #endif
438:     if (parse_command_modifiers(&ea, &errormsg, &cmdmod, FALSE) == FAIL)
439: 	goto doend;
440:     apply_cmdmod(&cmdmod);
441: #ifdef FEAT_EVAL
442:     vim9script = in_vim9script();
443: #endif
444:     after_modifier = ea.cmd;
445: 
446: #ifdef FEAT_EVAL
447:     ea.skip = did_emsg || got_int || did_throw || (cstack->cs_idx >= 0
448: 			 && !(cstack->cs_flags[cstack->cs_idx] & CSF_ACTIVE));
449: #else
450:     ea.skip = (if_level > 0);
451: #endif
452: 
453: /*
454:  * 3. Skip over the range to find the command.  Let "p" point to after it.
455:  *
456:  * We need the command to know what kind of range it uses.
457:  */
458:     cmd = ea.cmd;
459: #ifdef FEAT_EVAL
460:     // In Vim9 script a colon is required before the range.  This may also be
461:     // after command modifiers.
462:     if (vim9script && (flags & DOCMD_RANGEOK) == 0)
463:     {
464: 	may_have_range = FALSE;
465: 	for (p = ea.cmd; p >= *cmdlinep; --p)
466: 	{
467: 	    if (*p == ':')
468: 		may_have_range = TRUE;
469: 	    if (p < ea.cmd && !VIM_ISWHITE(*p))
470: 		break;
471: 	}
472:     }
473:     else
474: 	may_have_range = TRUE;
475:     if (may_have_range)
476: #endif
477: 	ea.cmd = skip_range(ea.cmd, TRUE, NULL);
478: 
479: #ifdef FEAT_EVAL
480:     if (vim9script && !may_have_range)
481:     {
482: 	if (ea.cmd == cmd + 1 && *cmd == '$')
483: 	    // should be "$VAR = val"
484: 	    --ea.cmd;
485: 	p = find_ex_command(&ea, NULL, lookup_scriptitem, NULL);
486: 	if (ea.cmdidx == CMD_SIZE)
487: 	{
488: 	    char_u *ar = skip_range(ea.cmd, TRUE, NULL);
489: 
490: 	    // If a ':' before the range is missing, give a clearer error
491: 	    // message.
492: 	    if (ar > ea.cmd && !ea.skip)
493: 	    {
494: 		semsg(_(e_colon_required_before_range_str), ea.cmd);
495: 		goto doend;
496: 	    }
497: 	}
498:     }
499:     else
500: #endif
501: 	p = find_ex_command(&ea, NULL, NULL, NULL);
502: 
503: #ifdef FEAT_EVAL
504: # ifdef FEAT_PROFILE
505:     // Count this line for profiling if skip is TRUE.
506:     if (do_profiling == PROF_YES
507: 	    && (!ea.skip || cstack->cs_idx == 0 || (cstack->cs_idx > 0
508: 		     && (cstack->cs_flags[cstack->cs_idx - 1] & CSF_ACTIVE))))
509:     {
510: 	int skip = did_emsg || got_int || did_throw;
511: 
512: 	if (ea.cmdidx == CMD_catch)
513: 	    skip = !skip && !(cstack->cs_idx >= 0
514: 			  && (cstack->cs_flags[cstack->cs_idx] & CSF_THROWN)
515: 			  && !(cstack->cs_flags[cstack->cs_idx] & CSF_CAUGHT));
516: 	else if (ea.cmdidx == CMD_else || ea.cmdidx == CMD_elseif)
517: 	    skip = skip || !(cstack->cs_idx >= 0
518: 			  && !(cstack->cs_flags[cstack->cs_idx]
519: 						  & (CSF_ACTIVE | CSF_TRUE)));
520: 	else if (ea.cmdidx == CMD_finally)
521: 	    skip = FALSE;
522: 	else if (ea.cmdidx != CMD_endif
523: 		&& ea.cmdidx != CMD_endfor
524: 		&& ea.cmdidx != CMD_endtry
525: 		&& ea.cmdidx != CMD_endwhile)
526: 	    skip = ea.skip;
527: 
528: 	if (!skip)
529: 	{
530: 	    if (getline_equal(fgetline, cookie, get_func_line))
531: 		func_line_exec(getline_cookie(fgetline, cookie));
532: 	    else if (getline_equal(fgetline, cookie, getsourceline))
533: 		script_line_exec();
534: 	}
535:     }
536: # endif
537: 
538:     // May go to debug mode.  If this happens and the ">quit" debug command is
539:     // used, throw an interrupt exception and skip the next command.
540:     dbg_check_breakpoint(&ea);
541:     if (!ea.skip && got_int)
542:     {
543: 	ea.skip = TRUE;
544: 	(void)do_intthrow(cstack);
545:     }
546: #endif
547: 
548: /*
549:  * 4. parse a range specifier of the form: addr [,addr] [;addr] ..
550:  *
551:  * where 'addr' is:
552:  *
553:  * %	      (entire file)
554:  * $  [+-NUM]
555:  * 'x [+-NUM] (where x denotes a currently defined mark)
556:  * .  [+-NUM]
557:  * [+-NUM]..
558:  * NUM
559:  *
560:  * The ea.cmd pointer is updated to point to the first character following the
561:  * range spec. If an initial address is found, but no second, the upper bound
562:  * is equal to the lower.
563:  */
564: 
565:     // ea.addr_type for user commands is set by find_ucmd
566:     if (!IS_USER_CMDIDX(ea.cmdidx))
567:     {
568: 	if (ea.cmdidx != CMD_SIZE)
569: 	    ea.addr_type = cmdnames[(int)ea.cmdidx].cmd_addr_type;
570: 	else
571: 	    ea.addr_type = ADDR_LINES;
572: 
573: 	// :wincmd range depends on the argument.
574: 	if (ea.cmdidx == CMD_wincmd && p != NULL)
575: 	    get_wincmd_addr_type(skipwhite(p), &ea);
576: #ifdef FEAT_QUICKFIX
577: 	// :.cc in quickfix window uses line number
578: 	if ((ea.cmdidx == CMD_cc || ea.cmdidx == CMD_ll) && bt_quickfix(curbuf))
579: 	    ea.addr_type = ADDR_OTHER;
580: #endif
581:     }
582: 
583:     ea.cmd = cmd;
584: #ifdef FEAT_EVAL
585:     if (!may_have_range)
586: 	ea.line1 = ea.line2 = default_address(&ea);
587:     else
588: #endif
589: 	if (parse_cmd_address(&ea, &errormsg, FALSE) == FAIL)
590: 	    goto doend;
591: 
592: /*
593:  * 5. Parse the command.
594:  */
595: 
596:     /*
597:      * Skip ':' and any white space
598:      */
599:     ea.cmd = skipwhite(ea.cmd);
600:     while (*ea.cmd == ':')
601: 	ea.cmd = skipwhite(ea.cmd + 1);
602: 
603:     /*
604:      * If we got a line, but no command, then go to the line.
605:      * If we find a '|' or '\n' we set ea.nextcmd.
606:      */
607:     if (*ea.cmd == NUL || comment_start(ea.cmd, starts_with_colon)
608: 			       || (ea.nextcmd = check_nextcmd(ea.cmd)) != NULL)
609:     {
610: 	/*
611: 	 * strange vi behaviour:
612: 	 * ":3"		jumps to line 3
613: 	 * ":3|..."	prints line 3  (not in Vim9 script)
614: 	 * ":|"		prints current line  (not in Vim9 script)
615: 	 */
616: 	if (ea.skip)	    // skip this if inside :if
617: 	    goto doend;
618: 	errormsg = ex_range_without_command(&ea);
619: 	goto doend;
620:     }
621: 
622:     // If this looks like an undefined user command and there are CmdUndefined
623:     // autocommands defined, trigger the matching autocommands.
624:     if (p != NULL && ea.cmdidx == CMD_SIZE && !ea.skip
625: 	    && ASCII_ISUPPER(*ea.cmd)
626: 	    && has_cmdundefined())
627:     {
628: 	int ret;
629: 
630: 	p = ea.cmd;
631: 	while (ASCII_ISALNUM(*p))
632: 	    ++p;
633: 	p = vim_strnsave(ea.cmd, p - ea.cmd);
634: 	ret = apply_autocmds(EVENT_CMDUNDEFINED, p, p, TRUE, NULL);
635: 	vim_free(p);
636: 	// If the autocommands did something and didn't cause an error, try
637: 	// finding the command again.
638: 	p = (ret
639: #ifdef FEAT_EVAL
640: 		&& !aborting()
641: #endif
642: 		) ? find_ex_command(&ea, NULL, NULL, NULL) : ea.cmd;
643:     }
644: 
645:     if (p == NULL)
646:     {
647: 	if (!ea.skip)
648: 	    errormsg = _(e_ambiguous_use_of_user_defined_command);
649: 	goto doend;
650:     }
651:     // Check for wrong commands.
652:     if (*p == '!' && ea.cmd[1] == 0151 && ea.cmd[0] == 78
653: 	    && !IS_USER_CMDIDX(ea.cmdidx))
654:     {
655: 	errormsg = uc_fun_cmd();
656: 	goto doend;
657:     }
658: 
659:     if (ea.cmdidx == CMD_SIZE)
660:     {
661: 	if (!ea.skip)
662: 	{
663: 	    STRCPY(IObuff, _(e_not_an_editor_command));
664: 	    if (!sourcing)
665: 	    {
666: 		// If the modifier was parsed OK the error must be in the
667: 		// following command
668: 		if (after_modifier != NULL)
669: 		    append_command(after_modifier);
670: 		else
671: 		    append_command(*cmdlinep);
672: 	    }
673: 	    errormsg = (char *)IObuff;
674: 	    did_emsg_syntax = TRUE;
675: 	}
676: 	goto doend;
677:     }
678: 
679:     ni = (!IS_USER_CMDIDX(ea.cmdidx)
680: 	    && (cmdnames[ea.cmdidx].cmd_func == ex_ni
681: #ifdef HAVE_EX_SCRIPT_NI
682: 	     || cmdnames[ea.cmdidx].cmd_func == ex_script_ni
683: #endif
684: 	     ));
685: 
686: #ifndef FEAT_EVAL
687:     /*
688:      * When the expression evaluation is disabled, recognize the ":if" and
689:      * ":endif" commands and ignore everything in between it.
690:      */
691:     if (ea.cmdidx == CMD_if)
692: 	++if_level;
693:     if (if_level)
694:     {
695: 	if (ea.cmdidx == CMD_endif)
696: 	    --if_level;
697: 	goto doend;
698:     }
699: 
700: #endif
701: 
702:     // forced commands
703:     if (*p == '!' && ea.cmdidx != CMD_substitute
704: 	    && ea.cmdidx != CMD_smagic && ea.cmdidx != CMD_snomagic)
705:     {
706: 	++p;
707: 	ea.forceit = TRUE;
708:     }
709:     else
710: 	ea.forceit = FALSE;
711: 
712: /*
713:  * 6. Parse arguments.  Then check for errors.
714:  */
715:     if (!IS_USER_CMDIDX(ea.cmdidx))
716: 	ea.argt = (long)cmdnames[(int)ea.cmdidx].cmd_argt;
717: 
718:     if (!ea.skip)
719:     {
720: #ifdef HAVE_SANDBOX
721: 	if (sandbox != 0 && !(ea.argt & EX_SBOXOK))
722: 	{
723: 	    // Command not allowed in sandbox.
724: 	    errormsg = _(e_not_allowed_in_sandbox);
725: 	    goto doend;
726: 	}
727: #endif
728: 	if (restricted != 0 && (ea.argt & EX_RESTRICT))
729: 	{
730: 	    errormsg = _(e_command_not_allowed_in_rvim);
731: 	    goto doend;
732: 	}
733: 	if (!curbuf->b_p_ma && (ea.argt & EX_MODIFY))
734: 	{
735: 	    // Command not allowed in non-'modifiable' buffer
736: 	    errormsg = _(e_cannot_make_changes_modifiable_is_off);
737: 	    goto doend;
738: 	}
739: 
740: 	if (!IS_USER_CMDIDX(ea.cmdidx))
741: 	{
742: #ifdef FEAT_CMDWIN
743: 	    if (cmdwin_type != 0 && !(ea.argt & EX_CMDWIN))
744: 	    {
745: 		// Command not allowed in the command line window
746: 		errormsg = _(e_invalid_in_cmdline_window);
747: 		goto doend;
748: 	    }
749: #endif
750: 	    if (text_locked() && !(ea.argt & EX_LOCK_OK))
751: 	    {
752: 		// Command not allowed when text is locked
753: 		errormsg = _(get_text_locked_msg());
754: 		goto doend;
755: 	    }
756: 	}
757: 
758: 	// Disallow editing another buffer when "curbuf_lock" is set.
759: 	// Do allow ":checktime" (it is postponed).
760: 	// Do allow ":edit" (check for an argument later).
761: 	// Do allow ":file" with no arguments (check for an argument later).
762: 	if (!(ea.argt & (EX_CMDWIN | EX_LOCK_OK))
763: 		&& ea.cmdidx != CMD_checktime
764: 		&& ea.cmdidx != CMD_edit
765: 		&& ea.cmdidx != CMD_file
766: 		&& !IS_USER_CMDIDX(ea.cmdidx)
767: 		&& curbuf_locked())
768: 	    goto doend;
769: 
770: 	if (!ni && !(ea.argt & EX_RANGE) && ea.addr_count > 0)
771: 	{
772: 	    errormsg = _(e_no_range_allowed);
773: 	    goto doend;
774: 	}
775:     }
776: 
777:     if (!ni && !(ea.argt & EX_BANG) && ea.forceit)
778:     {
779: 	errormsg = _(e_no_bang_allowed);
780: 	goto doend;
781:     }
782: 
783:     /*
784:      * Don't complain about the range if it is not used
785:      * (could happen if line_count is accidentally set to 0).
786:      */
787:     if (!ea.skip && !ni && (ea.argt & EX_RANGE))
788:     {
789: 	/*
790: 	 * If the range is backwards, ask for confirmation and, if given, swap
791: 	 * ea.line1 & ea.line2 so it's forwards again.
792: 	 * When global command is busy, don't ask, will fail below.
793: 	 */
794: 	if (!global_busy && ea.line1 > ea.line2)
795: 	{
796: 	    if (msg_silent == 0)
797: 	    {
798: 		if (sourcing || exmode_active)
799: 		{
800: 		    errormsg = _(e_backwards_range_given);
801: 		    goto doend;
802: 		}
803: 		if (ask_yesno((char_u *)
804: 			_("Backwards range given, OK to swap"), FALSE) != 'y')
805: 		    goto doend;
806: 	    }
807: 	    lnum = ea.line1;
808: 	    ea.line1 = ea.line2;
809: 	    ea.line2 = lnum;
810: 	}
811: 	if ((errormsg = invalid_range(&ea)) != NULL)
812: 	    goto doend;
813:     }
814: 
815:     if ((ea.addr_type == ADDR_OTHER) && ea.addr_count == 0)
816: 	// default is 1, not cursor
817: 	ea.line2 = 1;
818: 
819:     correct_range(&ea);
820: 
821: #ifdef FEAT_FOLDING
822:     if (((ea.argt & EX_WHOLEFOLD) || ea.addr_count >= 2) && !global_busy
823: 	    && ea.addr_type == ADDR_LINES)
824:     {
825: 	// Put the first line at the start of a closed fold, put the last line
826: 	// at the end of a closed fold.
827: 	(void)hasFolding(ea.line1, &ea.line1, NULL);
828: 	(void)hasFolding(ea.line2, NULL, &ea.line2);
829:     }
830: #endif
831: 
832: #ifdef FEAT_QUICKFIX
833:     /*
834:      * For the ":make" and ":grep" commands we insert the 'makeprg'/'grepprg'
835:      * option here, so things like % get expanded.
836:      */
837:     p = replace_makeprg(&ea, p, cmdlinep);
838:     if (p == NULL)
839: 	goto doend;
840: #endif
841: 
842:     /*
843:      * Skip to start of argument.
844:      * Don't do this for the ":!" command, because ":!! -l" needs the space.
845:      */
846:     if (ea.cmdidx == CMD_bang)
847: 	ea.arg = p;
848:     else
849: 	ea.arg = skipwhite(p);
850: 
851:     // ":file" cannot be run with an argument when "curbuf_lock" is set
852:     if (ea.cmdidx == CMD_file && *ea.arg != NUL && curbuf_locked())
853: 	goto doend;
854: 
855:     /*
856:      * Check for "++opt=val" argument.
857:      * Must be first, allow ":w ++enc=utf8 !cmd"
858:      */
859:     if (ea.argt & EX_ARGOPT)
860: 	while (ea.arg[0] == '+' && ea.arg[1] == '+')
861: 	    if (getargopt(&ea) == FAIL && !ni)
862: 	    {
863: 		errormsg = _(e_invalid_argument);
864: 		goto doend;
865: 	    }
866: 
867:     if (ea.cmdidx == CMD_write || ea.cmdidx == CMD_update)
868:     {
869: 	if (*ea.arg == '>')			// append
870: 	{
871: 	    if (*++ea.arg != '>')		// typed wrong
872: 	    {
873: 		errormsg = _(e_use_w_or_w_gt_gt);
874: 		goto doend;
875: 	    }
876: 	    ea.arg = skipwhite(ea.arg + 1);
877: 	    ea.append = TRUE;
878: 	}
879: 	else if (*ea.arg == '!' && ea.cmdidx == CMD_write)  // :w !filter
880: 	{
881: 	    ++ea.arg;
882: 	    ea.usefilter = TRUE;
883: 	}
884:     }
885: 
886:     if (ea.cmdidx == CMD_read)
887:     {
888: 	if (ea.forceit)
889: 	{
890: 	    ea.usefilter = TRUE;		// :r! filter if ea.forceit
891: 	    ea.forceit = FALSE;
892: 	}
893: 	else if (*ea.arg == '!')		// :r !filter
894: 	{
895: 	    ++ea.arg;
896: 	    ea.usefilter = TRUE;
897: 	}
898:     }
899: 
900:     if (ea.cmdidx == CMD_lshift || ea.cmdidx == CMD_rshift)
901:     {
902: 	ea.amount = 1;
903: 	while (*ea.arg == *ea.cmd)		// count number of '>' or '<'
904: 	{
905: 	    ++ea.arg;
906: 	    ++ea.amount;
907: 	}
908: 	ea.arg = skipwhite(ea.arg);
909:     }
910: 
911:     /*
912:      * Check for "+command" argument, before checking for next command.
913:      * Don't do this for ":read !cmd" and ":write !cmd".
914:      */
915:     if ((ea.argt & EX_CMDARG) && !ea.usefilter)
916: 	ea.do_ecmd_cmd = getargcmd(&ea.arg);
917: 
918:     /*
919:      * For commands that do not use '|' inside their argument: Check for '|' to
920:      * separate commands and '"' or '#' to start comments.
921:      *
922:      * Otherwise: Check for <newline> to end a shell command.
923:      * Also do this for ":read !cmd", ":write !cmd" and ":global".
924:      * Also do this inside a { - } block after :command and :autocmd.
925:      * Any others?
926:      */
927:     if ((ea.argt & EX_TRLBAR) && !ea.usefilter)
928:     {
929: 	separate_nextcmd(&ea, FALSE);
930:     }
931:     else if (ea.cmdidx == CMD_bang
932: 	    || ea.cmdidx == CMD_terminal
933: 	    || ea.cmdidx == CMD_global
934: 	    || ea.cmdidx == CMD_vglobal
935: 	    || ea.usefilter
936: #ifdef FEAT_EVAL
937: 	    || inside_block(&ea)
938: #endif
939: 	    )

247:     cmd_addr_T	cmd_addr_type;

341: static char_u	*do_one_cmd(char_u **, int, char_u *(*fgetline)(int, void *, int, getline_opt_T), void *cookie);

346:     static void
347: append_command(char_u *cmd)
348: {
349:     size_t  len = STRLEN(IObuff);
350:     char_u  *s = cmd;
351:     char_u  *d;
352: 
353:     if (len > IOSIZE - 100)
354:     {
355: 	// Not enough space, truncate and put in "...".
356: 	d = IObuff + IOSIZE - 100;
357: 	d -= mb_head_off(IObuff, d);
358: 	STRCPY(d, "...");
359:     }
360: 
361:     STRCAT(IObuff, ": ");
362:     d = IObuff + STRLEN(IObuff);
363:     while (*s != NUL && d - IObuff + 5 < IOSIZE)
364:     {
365: 	if (enc_utf8 ? (s[0] == 0xc2 && s[1] == 0xa0) : *s == 0xa0)
366: 	{
367: 	    s += enc_utf8 ? 2 : 1;
368: 	    STRCPY(d, "<a0>");
369: 	    d += 4;
370: 	}
371: 	else if (d - IObuff + (*mb_ptr2len)(s) + 1 >= IOSIZE)
372: 	    break;
373: 	else
374: 	    MB_COPY_CHAR(s, d);
375:     }
376:     *d = NUL;
377: }

379:     static char_u *
380: do_one_cmd(
381:     char_u	**cmdlinep,
382:     int		flags,
383: #ifdef FEAT_EVAL
384:     cstack_T	*cstack,
385: #endif
386:     char_u	*(*fgetline)(int, void *, int, getline_opt_T),
387:     void	*cookie)		// argument for fgetline()
388: {
389:     char_u	*p;
390:     linenr_T	lnum;
391:     long	n;
392:     char	*errormsg = NULL;	// error message
393:     char_u	*after_modifier = NULL;
394:     exarg_T	ea;			// Ex command arguments
395:     cmdmod_T	save_cmdmod;
396:     int		save_reg_executing = reg_executing;
397:     int		save_pending_end_reg_executing = pending_end_reg_executing;
398:     int		ni;			// set when Not Implemented
399:     char_u	*cmd;
400:     int		starts_with_colon = FALSE;
401: #ifdef FEAT_EVAL
402:     int		may_have_range;
403:     int		vim9script;
404:     int		did_set_expr_line = FALSE;
405: #endif
406:     int		sourcing = flags & DOCMD_VERBOSE;
407: 
408:     CLEAR_FIELD(ea);
409:     ea.line1 = 1;
410:     ea.line2 = 1;
411: #ifdef FEAT_EVAL
412:     ++ex_nesting_level;
413: #endif
414: 
415:     // When the last file has not been edited :q has to be typed twice.
416:     if (quitmore
417: #ifdef FEAT_EVAL
418: 	    // avoid that a function call in 'statusline' does this
419: 	    && !getline_equal(fgetline, cookie, get_func_line)
420: #endif
421: 	    // avoid that an autocommand, e.g. QuitPre, does this
422: 	    && !getline_equal(fgetline, cookie, getnextac))
423: 	--quitmore;
424: 
425:     /*
426:      * Reset browse, confirm, etc..  They are restored when returning, for
427:      * recursive calls.
428:      */
429:     save_cmdmod = cmdmod;
430: 
431:     // "#!anything" is handled like a comment.
432:     if ((*cmdlinep)[0] == '#' && (*cmdlinep)[1] == '!')
433: 	goto doend;
434: 
435: /*
436:  * 1. Skip comment lines and leading white space and colons.
437:  * 2. Handle command modifiers.
438:  */
439:     // The "ea" structure holds the arguments that can be used.
440:     ea.cmd = *cmdlinep;
441:     ea.cmdlinep = cmdlinep;
442:     ea.getline = fgetline;
443:     ea.cookie = cookie;
444: #ifdef FEAT_EVAL
445:     ea.cstack = cstack;
446:     starts_with_colon = *skipwhite(ea.cmd) == ':';
447: #endif
448:     if (parse_command_modifiers(&ea, &errormsg, &cmdmod, FALSE) == FAIL)
449: 	goto doend;
450:     apply_cmdmod(&cmdmod);
451: #ifdef FEAT_EVAL
452:     vim9script = in_vim9script();
453: #endif
454:     after_modifier = ea.cmd;
455: 
456: #ifdef FEAT_EVAL
457:     ea.skip = did_emsg || got_int || did_throw || (cstack->cs_idx >= 0
458: 			 && !(cstack->cs_flags[cstack->cs_idx] & CSF_ACTIVE));
459: #else
460:     ea.skip = (if_level > 0);
461: #endif
462: 
463: /*
464:  * 3. Skip over the range to find the command.  Let "p" point to after it.
465:  *
466:  * We need the command to know what kind of range it uses.
467:  */
468:     cmd = ea.cmd;
469: #ifdef FEAT_EVAL
470:     // In Vim9 script a colon is required before the range.  This may also be
471:     // after command modifiers.
472:     if (vim9script && (flags & DOCMD_RANGEOK) == 0)
473:     {
474: 	may_have_range = FALSE;
475: 	for (p = ea.cmd; p >= *cmdlinep; --p)
476: 	{
477: 	    if (*p == ':')
478: 		may_have_range = TRUE;
479: 	    if (p < ea.cmd && !VIM_ISWHITE(*p))
480: 		break;
481: 	}
482:     }
483:     else
484: 	may_have_range = TRUE;
485:     if (may_have_range)
486: #endif
487: 	ea.cmd = skip_range(ea.cmd, TRUE, NULL);
488: 
489: #ifdef FEAT_EVAL
490:     if (vim9script && !may_have_range)
491:     {
492: 	if (ea.cmd == cmd + 1 && *cmd == '$')
493: 	    // should be "$VAR = val"
494: 	    --ea.cmd;
495: 	p = find_ex_command(&ea, NULL, lookup_scriptitem, NULL);
496: 	if (ea.cmdidx == CMD_SIZE)
497: 	{
498: 	    char_u *ar = skip_range(ea.cmd, TRUE, NULL);
499: 
500: 	    // If a ':' before the range is missing, give a clearer error
501: 	    // message.
502: 	    if (ar > ea.cmd && !ea.skip)
503: 	    {
504: 		semsg(_(e_colon_required_before_range_str), ea.cmd);
505: 		goto doend;
506: 	    }
507: 	}
508:     }
509:     else
510: #endif
511: 	p = find_ex_command(&ea, NULL, NULL, NULL);
512: 
513: #ifdef FEAT_EVAL
514: # ifdef FEAT_PROFILE
515:     // Count this line for profiling if skip is TRUE.
516:     if (do_profiling == PROF_YES
517: 	    && (!ea.skip || cstack->cs_idx == 0 || (cstack->cs_idx > 0
518: 		     && (cstack->cs_flags[cstack->cs_idx - 1] & CSF_ACTIVE))))
519:     {
520: 	int skip = did_emsg || got_int || did_throw;
521: 
522: 	if (ea.cmdidx == CMD_catch)
523: 	    skip = !skip && !(cstack->cs_idx >= 0
524: 			  && (cstack->cs_flags[cstack->cs_idx] & CSF_THROWN)
525: 			  && !(cstack->cs_flags[cstack->cs_idx] & CSF_CAUGHT));
526: 	else if (ea.cmdidx == CMD_else || ea.cmdidx == CMD_elseif)
527: 	    skip = skip || !(cstack->cs_idx >= 0
528: 			  && !(cstack->cs_flags[cstack->cs_idx]
529: 						  & (CSF_ACTIVE | CSF_TRUE)));
530: 	else if (ea.cmdidx == CMD_finally)
531: 	    skip = FALSE;
532: 	else if (ea.cmdidx != CMD_endif
533: 		&& ea.cmdidx != CMD_endfor
534: 		&& ea.cmdidx != CMD_endtry
535: 		&& ea.cmdidx != CMD_endwhile)
536: 	    skip = ea.skip;
537: 
538: 	if (!skip)
539: 	{
540: 	    if (getline_equal(fgetline, cookie, get_func_line))
541: 		func_line_exec(getline_cookie(fgetline, cookie));
542: 	    else if (getline_equal(fgetline, cookie, getsourceline))
543: 		script_line_exec();
544: 	}
545:     }
546: # endif
547: 
548:     // May go to debug mode.  If this happens and the ">quit" debug command is
549:     // used, throw an interrupt exception and skip the next command.
550:     dbg_check_breakpoint(&ea);
551:     if (!ea.skip && got_int)
552:     {
553: 	ea.skip = TRUE;
554: 	(void)do_intthrow(cstack);
555:     }
556: #endif
557: 
558: /*
559:  * 4. parse a range specifier of the form: addr [,addr] [;addr] ..
560:  *
561:  * where 'addr' is:
562:  *
563:  * %	      (entire file)
564:  * $  [+-NUM]
565:  * 'x [+-NUM] (where x denotes a currently defined mark)
566:  * .  [+-NUM]
567:  * [+-NUM]..
568:  * NUM
569:  *
570:  * The ea.cmd pointer is updated to point to the first character following the
571:  * range spec. If an initial address is found, but no second, the upper bound
572:  * is equal to the lower.
573:  */
574: 
575:     // ea.addr_type for user commands is set by find_ucmd
576:     if (!IS_USER_CMDIDX(ea.cmdidx))
577:     {
578: 	if (ea.cmdidx != CMD_SIZE)
579: 	    ea.addr_type = cmdnames[(int)ea.cmdidx].cmd_addr_type;
580: 	else
581: 	    ea.addr_type = ADDR_LINES;
582: 
583: 	// :wincmd range depends on the argument.
584: 	if (ea.cmdidx == CMD_wincmd && p != NULL)
585: 	    get_wincmd_addr_type(skipwhite(p), &ea);
586: #ifdef FEAT_QUICKFIX
587: 	// :.cc in quickfix window uses line number
588: 	if ((ea.cmdidx == CMD_cc || ea.cmdidx == CMD_ll) && bt_quickfix(curbuf))
589: 	    ea.addr_type = ADDR_OTHER;
590: #endif
591:     }
592: 
593:     ea.cmd = cmd;
594: #ifdef FEAT_EVAL
595:     if (!may_have_range)
596: 	ea.line1 = ea.line2 = default_address(&ea);
597:     else
598: #endif
599: 	if (parse_cmd_address(&ea, &errormsg, FALSE) == FAIL)
600: 	    goto doend;
601: 
602: /*
603:  * 5. Parse the command.
604:  */
605: 
606:     /*
607:      * Skip ':' and any white space
608:      */
609:     ea.cmd = skipwhite(ea.cmd);
610:     while (*ea.cmd == ':')
611: 	ea.cmd = skipwhite(ea.cmd + 1);
612: 
613:     /*
614:      * If we got a line, but no command, then go to the line.
615:      * If we find a '|' or '\n' we set ea.nextcmd.
616:      */
617:     if (*ea.cmd == NUL || comment_start(ea.cmd, starts_with_colon)
618: 			       || (ea.nextcmd = check_nextcmd(ea.cmd)) != NULL)
619:     {
620: 	/*
621: 	 * strange vi behaviour:
622: 	 * ":3"		jumps to line 3
623: 	 * ":3|..."	prints line 3  (not in Vim9 script)
624: 	 * ":|"		prints current line  (not in Vim9 script)
625: 	 */
626: 	if (ea.skip)	    // skip this if inside :if
627: 	    goto doend;
628: 	errormsg = ex_range_without_command(&ea);
629: 	goto doend;
630:     }
631: 
632:     // If this looks like an undefined user command and there are CmdUndefined
633:     // autocommands defined, trigger the matching autocommands.
634:     if (p != NULL && ea.cmdidx == CMD_SIZE && !ea.skip
635: 	    && ASCII_ISUPPER(*ea.cmd)
636: 	    && has_cmdundefined())
637:     {
638: 	int ret;
639: 
640: 	p = ea.cmd;
641: 	while (ASCII_ISALNUM(*p))
642: 	    ++p;
643: 	p = vim_strnsave(ea.cmd, p - ea.cmd);
644: 	ret = apply_autocmds(EVENT_CMDUNDEFINED, p, p, TRUE, NULL);
645: 	vim_free(p);
646: 	// If the autocommands did something and didn't cause an error, try
647: 	// finding the command again.
648: 	p = (ret
649: #ifdef FEAT_EVAL
650: 		&& !aborting()
651: #endif
652: 		) ? find_ex_command(&ea, NULL, NULL, NULL) : ea.cmd;
653:     }
654: 
655:     if (p == NULL)
656:     {
657: 	if (!ea.skip)
658: 	    errormsg = _(e_ambiguous_use_of_user_defined_command);
659: 	goto doend;
660:     }
661:     // Check for wrong commands.
662:     if (*p == '!' && ea.cmd[1] == 0151 && ea.cmd[0] == 78
663: 	    && !IS_USER_CMDIDX(ea.cmdidx))
664:     {
665: 	errormsg = uc_fun_cmd();
666: 	goto doend;
667:     }
668: 
669:     if (ea.cmdidx == CMD_SIZE)
670:     {
671: 	if (!ea.skip)
672: 	{
673: 	    STRCPY(IObuff, _(e_not_an_editor_command));
674: 	    if (!sourcing)
675: 	    {
676: 		// If the modifier was parsed OK the error must be in the
677: 		// following command
678: 		if (after_modifier != NULL)
679: 		    append_command(after_modifier);
680: 		else
681: 		    append_command(*cmdlinep);
682: 	    }
683: 	    errormsg = (char *)IObuff;
684: 	    did_emsg_syntax = TRUE;
685: 	}
686: 	goto doend;
687:     }
688: 
689:     ni = (!IS_USER_CMDIDX(ea.cmdidx)
690: 	    && (cmdnames[ea.cmdidx].cmd_func == ex_ni
691: #ifdef HAVE_EX_SCRIPT_NI
692: 	     || cmdnames[ea.cmdidx].cmd_func == ex_script_ni
693: #endif
694: 	     ));
695: 
696: #ifndef FEAT_EVAL
697:     /*
698:      * When the expression evaluation is disabled, recognize the ":if" and
699:      * ":endif" commands and ignore everything in between it.
700:      */
701:     if (ea.cmdidx == CMD_if)
702: 	++if_level;
703:     if (if_level)
704:     {
705: 	if (ea.cmdidx == CMD_endif)
706: 	    --if_level;
707: 	goto doend;
708:     }
709: 
710: #endif
711: 
712:     // forced commands
713:     if (*p == '!' && ea.cmdidx != CMD_substitute
714: 	    && ea.cmdidx != CMD_smagic && ea.cmdidx != CMD_snomagic)
715:     {
716: 	++p;
717: 	ea.forceit = TRUE;
718:     }
719:     else
720: 	ea.forceit = FALSE;
721: 
722: /*
723:  * 6. Parse arguments.  Then check for errors.
724:  */
725:     if (!IS_USER_CMDIDX(ea.cmdidx))
726: 	ea.argt = (long)cmdnames[(int)ea.cmdidx].cmd_argt;
727: 
728:     if (!ea.skip)
729:     {
730: #ifdef HAVE_SANDBOX
731: 	if (sandbox != 0 && !(ea.argt & EX_SBOXOK))
732: 	{
733: 	    // Command not allowed in sandbox.
734: 	    errormsg = _(e_not_allowed_in_sandbox);
735: 	    goto doend;
736: 	}
737: #endif
738: 	if (restricted != 0 && (ea.argt & EX_RESTRICT))
739: 	{
740: 	    errormsg = _(e_command_not_allowed_in_rvim);
741: 	    goto doend;
742: 	}
743: 	if (!curbuf->b_p_ma && (ea.argt & EX_MODIFY))
744: 	{
745: 	    // Command not allowed in non-'modifiable' buffer
746: 	    errormsg = _(e_cannot_make_changes_modifiable_is_off);
747: 	    goto doend;
748: 	}
749: 
750: 	if (!IS_USER_CMDIDX(ea.cmdidx))
751: 	{
752: #ifdef FEAT_CMDWIN
753: 	    if (cmdwin_type != 0 && !(ea.argt & EX_CMDWIN))
754: 	    {
755: 		// Command not allowed in the command line window
756: 		errormsg = _(e_invalid_in_cmdline_window);
757: 		goto doend;
758: 	    }
759: #endif
760: 	    if (text_locked() && !(ea.argt & EX_LOCK_OK))
761: 	    {
762: 		// Command not allowed when text is locked
763: 		errormsg = _(get_text_locked_msg());
764: 		goto doend;
765: 	    }
766: 	}
767: 
768: 	// Disallow editing another buffer when "curbuf_lock" is set.
769: 	// Do allow ":checktime" (it is postponed).
770: 	// Do allow ":edit" (check for an argument later).
771: 	// Do allow ":file" with no arguments (check for an argument later).
772: 	if (!(ea.argt & (EX_CMDWIN | EX_LOCK_OK))
773: 		&& ea.cmdidx != CMD_checktime
774: 		&& ea.cmdidx != CMD_edit
775: 		&& ea.cmdidx != CMD_file
776: 		&& !IS_USER_CMDIDX(ea.cmdidx)
777: 		&& curbuf_locked())
778: 	    goto doend;
779: 
780: 	if (!ni && !(ea.argt & EX_RANGE) && ea.addr_count > 0)
781: 	{
782: 	    errormsg = _(e_no_range_allowed);
783: 	    goto doend;
784: 	}
785:     }
786: 
787:     if (!ni && !(ea.argt & EX_BANG) && ea.forceit)
788:     {
789: 	errormsg = _(e_no_bang_allowed);
790: 	goto doend;
791:     }
792: 
793:     /*
794:      * Don't complain about the range if it is not used
795:      * (could happen if line_count is accidentally set to 0).
796:      */
797:     if (!ea.skip && !ni && (ea.argt & EX_RANGE))
798:     {
799: 	/*
800: 	 * If the range is backwards, ask for confirmation and, if given, swap
801: 	 * ea.line1 & ea.line2 so it's forwards again.
802: 	 * When global command is busy, don't ask, will fail below.
803: 	 */
804: 	if (!global_busy && ea.line1 > ea.line2)
805: 	{
806: 	    if (msg_silent == 0)
807: 	    {
808: 		if (sourcing || exmode_active)
809: 		{
810: 		    errormsg = _(e_backwards_range_given);
811: 		    goto doend;
812: 		}
813: 		if (ask_yesno((char_u *)
814: 			_("Backwards range given, OK to swap"), FALSE) != 'y')
815: 		    goto doend;
816: 	    }
817: 	    lnum = ea.line1;
818: 	    ea.line1 = ea.line2;
819: 	    ea.line2 = lnum;
820: 	}
